calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Bifurcated Issue Regarding Appointment Of Elisor And Title Of Real Property
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Bifurcated Issue Re: Appointing "Elisor" (See Order To Accept Resignation Filed This Date)
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terrence Heath for (petitioner) Estelita Napata, the former trustee and the present trustee.

The Court stated that we are here on an Order to Show Cause re preliminary injunction issued with a temporary restraining order and remnants of the previous petition. Mr. Heath stated that they would like to vacate the Order to Show Cause and temporary restraining order and dismiss the remaining request for an Elisor. The Court confirmed that the prior trustee is cooperating, vacated the temporary restraining order and Order to Show Cause and took the remaining petition off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts . (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-15-2015 Vacated

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Continued To Dec-15-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To (1) Accept Resignation Of Trustee, (2) Appoint New Trustee, (3) Determine Questions Of Construction Or, In The Alternative, Modify Trust, And (4) Appoint "Elisor"
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terrance Heath for (petitioner/trustee) Estelita Napata.

The Court stated that there was an issue with the proof of service, that we now have it but that it was served by the new trustee at her own residence. Mr. Heath stated that 2 of the children live there and that the other children are in and out of the home. The Court stated that it is irregular to have someone so close provide service, that it has received proper verification and that it would have liked to have had the children served differently but they have been served. The Court further stated that in regards to the appointment of Juana as the new trustee the trust forbids it, that the consents of the beneficiaries are not effective due to the spendthrift clause, that things have changed to allow for it to be modified, that it is inclined to allow for it to be modified to allow Juana to serve as long as she is not the current wife, 1.4 notwithstanding, asked about the status of the bankruptcy and that in regards to the request for elisor the TRO states no conveyance. Mr. Heath stated that the bankruptcy has been dismissed and asked if the preliminary injunction hearing transpires in December and the TRO is lifted would the court issue the conveyance. The Court stated that it would look for alternatives, that it is prepared to accept the resignation, approve the modification and confirm appointment of the new trustee and bifurcate the requests regarding the property and continue it out. The Court granted the petition to (1) accept resignation of trustee, etc. (except for the bifurcated issue of elisor)(subject to review of the order). The bifurcated issue of elisor and title of real property was continued to December 15, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Trustee; Appoint Successor Trustee; Order Accounting; Order Conveyance Or Transfer Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-29-2013 Continued To Mar-12-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trent Thornely for Petitioners.

The Petition to Remove Trustee; Appoint Successor Trustee, etc. was continued to March 12, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a Petition to Confirm Settlement Agreement and a stipulation regarding cancellation of the deed. The February 15, 2013 trial date was vacated.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Anasse.

Petition To Remove Trustee; Appoint Successor Trustee; Order Accounting; Order Conveyance Or Transfer Of Real Property
Off Calendar Nov-27-2012 Continued To Jan-29-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trent Thornley for and with Ricardo Pascubello, Matthew Pascubello (Beneficiaries/Petitioners). Ricardo Pascubello Sr. (Trustor). Juana Pascubello (Proposed Successor Trustee/Guardian Ad Litem for Christopher). Sally Victorian (Trustee). John Tran (Friend).

Mr. Thornley reported that they have reached a settlement agreement with Hal Wright (Counsel for Ms. Victorian) and that Ms. Victorian did not sign the stipulation. Ms. Victorian stated that she does not know why her attorney is not in court and that she did not do anything wrong and that she was instructed to transfer title of the house to obtain a loan. Discussions were held with regard to title of the house, tax issues and appointment of Ricardo, Sr. as successor co-trustees. Ms. Victorian argued that the Trust precluded Juana Victorian to act as Trustee. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation and advised Ms. Victorian to get counsel if Mr. Wright is no longer representing her. Mediation must be completed no later than January 25, 2013. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 25, 2013. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on January 29, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation. Mr. Thornley argued that the house belongs in the Trust. Ms. Victorian stated that she has no problem with putting the property into to the Trust. The Court advised parties to look into transactions that will have tax benefits. Trial date is set on February 15, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Harris.
